stemHtml: An experienced pilot of an aircraft with passengers on board is flying an instrument approach. The flight is on schedule, and the landing will be made according to the flight plan. When the pilot started the approach, the vertical visibility reduced to below the minima. He/she decides to continue the approach and the aircraft lands safely. Which statement is correct for this scenario?

options.a: The pilot thought the visibility might improve again, so continuing the approach was the correct action.
options.b: The extra risk was acceptable as the pilot was able to continue the approach and landed safely.
options.c: The pressure to land on time influenced the pilot to incorrectly decide to continue.
options.d: The pilot’s experience meant that his/her decision to break the rules was correct.
